name: The Classified Advertisement
axiom: Holmes's use of the personal columns was published as a method for reaching clients or suspects. This is accurate for approximately 60% of the published advertisements. The remaining 40% were communications in a cipher Watson understood but never decoded in the published accounts. They were communications between Holmes and the members of the network that managed the consequences of unspeakable correct inferences — the people who received the information that could not be published.
flavor_line: Seven advertisements in the published record begin with the phrase 'Found: a small item.' Holmes never found anything small. Watson always read these aloud to Holmes at breakfast. Holmes never responded.

surface: The personal column of a London newspaper, 1891. An advertisement beginning 'Found: a small item.'
Holmes listening at breakfast, not responding.
iceberg.echo: The classified advertisements are the Pane's postal system for the information that cannot be stated: a network of individuals who receive, via cipher, the inferences that cannot be published, and who act on them without attribution.
deep: The network that receives the unspeakable inferences is not Mycroft's government network — it is parallel, unofficial, and composed of people who need the information to prevent specific consequences
The 'Found: a small item' cipher signals a new inference has been deposited in the network's dead-drop system — a system that used the personal columns as its public-facing communication mechanism

Holmes never responded to these at breakfast because he could not: Watson was present, Watson did not know about the network, and acknowledging the advertisements in Watson's presence would have required an explanation Watson should not have

The buried weight: Watson read these advertisements to Holmes for years, in good faith, as ordinary items of interest in the morning paper. Holmes listened with the specific quality of someone receiving a confirmation he cannot acknowledge.

The network predates the Holmes-Watson partnership: Holmes established it before Watson arrived at 221B, which means Watson was brought into a domestic arrangement that already had this parallel infrastructure

Some network members are identified in the published canon under false names: the recurring 'informants' who provide Holmes with street-level intelligence are, in some cases, members of the inference-distribution network

The seven 'Found: a small item' advertisements span 1887 to 1903 — the full active period of the partnership. After 1903, the cipher changes. Watson stopped reading the personal columns to Holmes in 1903.

The reason Watson stopped: in 1903 he decoded one of the advertisements by accident, understood what it was, and never mentioned it to Holmes. Holmes knew Watson had decoded it. They did not discuss this either.

lampblack.gesture: Holding a newspaper at the breakfast table, reading aloud a specific small advertisement — not knowing what it is
lampblack.cosmological_relation: The unwitting relay: the figure who has been carrying the cipher-communications of the unspeakable-inference network without knowing, for years, that that is what he was doing
lampblack.wound: The 1903 accidental decode. Watson understood, in a single moment of reading, what the seven 'Found: a small item' advertisements were, what the network was, and what information had been moving through him for years. He folded the paper. He did not read Holmes any more personal columns after that.
lampblack.forbidden_act: Telling Holmes he decoded it — which would require Holmes to either confirm the network's existence or deny it, both of which Watson understood would damage the partnership in ways that silence would not
lampblack.role: Watson as unwitting network relay — the partnership's most intimate non-knowledge
lampblack.silhouette: A figure at a breakfast table, holding a newspaper, reading aloud — the domestic posture of morning ritual
lampblack.prop: The folded newspaper — the cipher's public carrier

contraband_relation_note: The classified advertisement mediates between the unspeakable correct inference and its necessary consequence — it is the mechanism by which the contraband moves without being named.

name: The Hiatus, from the Other Side
axiom: Watson published the Hiatus as a death. This was not a lie. Watson believed Holmes was dead for approximately fourteen months. What Watson did not publish was what he did with the archive of unspeakable correct inferences during those fourteen months — the period when he was the only living custodian of information whose custodian was supposed to be dead.
flavor_line: Watson burned four notebooks in the fourteen months. He kept three. He has never written about what distinguished the ones he burned from the ones he kept.

surface: Four notebooks, burned. Three, kept. The division Watson has never explained.
The kept notebooks are on a shelf in Watson's consulting room. He does not open them.
iceberg.echo: The Hiatus, from Watson's side, is the Pane's central test of the archive-custodian function: what do you do with the unspeakable correct inferences when the deducer is gone?
deep: Watson's fourteen-month custodianship was not passive grief but active management: the archive of unspeakable inferences required maintenance that Holmes had been providing nightly

Without Holmes's nightly maintenance, the archive's integrity began to degrade: Watson found, over the fourteen months, that the inferences were beginning to resolve — beginning to become speakable, to attach to present events, to demand action

The four burned notebooks contained inferences that had resolved into actionability: Watson burned them because acting on them would have required identifying himself as the custodian of information he was not supposed to have

The three kept notebooks contained inferences that were still structurally stable — still purely historical, still without present consequence. Watson kept them because they were the most important inferences, the ones that could not be destroyed without losing the evidence they contained.

The buried weight: the distinction Watson has never written about (burned vs. kept) is the distinction between actionable and archival. He made this distinction alone, without Holmes, and it required him to become a different kind of custodian than he had been.

Holmes returned after fourteen months. Watson showed him the three kept notebooks. Holmes read them. Then he took them out of the consulting room shelf and put them somewhere Watson has not been able to find since.

Watson has not asked where they are. He knows Holmes moved them for a reason he would not explain. This is the most extreme application of the no-discussion protocol: extending it to Watson's own custody decisions.

The fourteen months changed Watson in a way the published accounts do not record: he emerged from the Hiatus with a different understanding of what the archive was and why it required two custodians. He has never been comfortable as a single point of failure since.

lampblack.gesture: Holding an open notebook over a fireplace, deciding — a decision that has already been made but has not yet been executed
lampblack.cosmological_relation: The solo custodian: the figure managing the archive alone during the period designed for a two-person system, discovering in the process what the two-person system was actually for
lampblack.wound: Burned the wrong notebook first. Understood this six weeks later when an event occurred that the burned notebook had contained the correct inference for. Had to watch the event unfold without the ability to act.
lampblack.forbidden_act: Publishing any account of the fourteen-month custodianship — which would require explaining what was in the burned notebooks, which would require stating inferences that Watson burned precisely because they could not be stated
lampblack.role: Watson as solo archive-custodian — the partnership's most strained form
lampblack.silhouette: A figure holding an open notebook over a small fireplace, very still — in the moment of decision before burning
lampblack.prop: The open notebook, edge toward the fire — the moment of irreversible decision

contraband_relation_note: The Hiatus custody exposes the Pane's central contraband structure by forcing Watson to manage it alone — the absence of the deducer reveals the full architecture of what was being managed.

name: The Final Problem, Unwitnessed
axiom: Watson was not at Reichenbach. Watson arrived at the falls and found the note. The falls is the one case in the canon where the central event has no witness. This is not incidental. The final problem required an unwitnessed resolution specifically because its resolution involved stating, in action rather than in words, the most unspeakable correct inference in the archive — the one that could only be expressed by removing its deducer from the world in which stating it would have been possible.
flavor_line: Watson read the note. He understood everything. He published half of what he understood. The other half is in the note he has never shown anyone.

surface: A note at the edge of the falls. Watson reading it.
A second note, in Watson's pocket, that Watson wrote after reading the first.
iceberg.echo: The final problem is the Pane's terminal event: the one where the archive-pressure exceeded the management capacity, and the resolution required removing the archive from the context in which it could become catastrophic.
deep: Holmes's fall — real or staged — was an act of cosmological architecture: it removed the primary custodian of the most dangerous inferences from the context in which those inferences could be acted upon

The unwitnessed resolution is essential: a witnessed resolution would have required either Holmes or Moriarty to speak the inference in the moment of resolution, which was what the resolution was designed to prevent

Watson's second note (the one he never showed anyone) contains what he understood immediately upon reading Holmes's note — the reasoning, the archive-pressure, the inference that had driven Holmes to this resolution

The buried weight: Watson understood the inference correctly. This is the most important fact in the Pane. Watson, reading a suicide note at the edge of a waterfall, correctly deduced the unspeakable correct inference that had driven Holmes there. And chose not to publish it.
The 'half of what he understood' that Watson published: the grief, the partnership, the sense of loss, the tribute to a great mind. The correct and publishable content.
The 'other half': the inference, the archive, the fourteen-month custodianship that followed, and Watson's understanding of why Holmes had to resolve it this way.
Holmes returned. This means the resolution was staged — the inference was not removed from the world, only from the active London context. The Hiatus was a hibernation of the archive.

The final problem is the Pane's most load-bearing event because it reveals the maximum: the archive has a pressure limit, the management system can fail, and the correct response to a failed management system is removal rather than disclosure. Watson has known this since Reichenbach. He has never published it.

lampblack.gesture: Standing at the edge of a waterfall, holding a note, looking at the falls — then reaching into a pocket for a second piece of paper to write on
lampblack.cosmological_relation: The terminal witness-surrogate: the figure who arrives at the unwitnessed event and, by reading correctly, becomes the only witness to what was not witnessed — the deduction about the event rather than the event itself
lampblack.wound: The second note, which Watson has kept for forty years, which contains the correct understanding of why Holmes chose this resolution. The note is in his handwriting. It is the one document in his possession that he knows must be destroyed before he dies, and which he cannot bring himself to destroy.
lampblack.forbidden_act: Publishing the second note — which would require publishing the correct understanding, which is the most unspeakable correct inference Watson has ever produced, deduced alone at a waterfall in a moment of grief
lampblack.role: Watson at Reichenbach — the Pane's final witness-surrogate, the figure who deduced what was not meant to be deduced
lampblack.silhouette: A figure at the edge of a mountain waterfall, holding a note, one hand reaching toward a coat pocket — the moment between reading and writing
lampblack.prop: The first note, held; the second piece of paper, about to be reached for

contraband_relation_note: Watson carries the Pane's ultimate contraband: the correct understanding of the final problem, written in his own hand, which has been in his pocket for forty years and which he has told no one.
